description: Two LLM iterations modeling the effect-checker's par-conflict path. Iteration 0 is the LLM's natural attempt: a module-level `let mut SENT_COUNT` counter incremented from inside a `par { }` block. E0408 fires from the effect phase (no machine-applicable `replacement` — descriptive). Iteration 1 lifts the counter into a `par struct Counter { count: Atomic[i64] }` passed by `ref` and increments via `fetch_add`; build clean with the parallelism preserved. The harness should NOT find a `replacement` field (no karac fix invocation) — convergence relies on the LLM reading the E0408 message and restructuring to a concurrency primitive.

responses.note: LLM's first attempt — a plain shared module-level counter written from inside par { }. E0408 fires: 'module-level let mut SENT_COUNT cannot be written from inside par { } — wrap in Atomic[T], Mutex[T], or use #[thread_local]'. The counter is named SCREAMING_SNAKE so the only diagnostic is the concurrency one (module-level bindings are Const-class; a Value-class name would also trip E_MODULE_BINDING_NAMING).

After receiving E0408, the LLM lifts the counter into a `par struct Counter { count: Atomic[i64] }`, passes it by `ref`, and increments via `fetch_add`. The par { } block is preserved — the fix made the counter race-free, it did NOT serialize the work. Build clean.
